prediction.reasoning: Based on typical stock volatility patterns for an 18-day horizon, I applied a random walk model with drift close to zero. Using historical equity volatility of approximately 20-25% annualized, the daily standard deviation is roughly 1.3-1.6%. Over 18 days, this compounds to about 5.5-6.8% standard deviation. The point estimate assumes minimal drift, with confidence intervals constructed using normal distribution assumptions at 0.67, 1.28, and 1.64 standard deviations respectively.
